title: Managing the impacts of EU electric vehicle import tariffs
abstract: Published online: 10 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02102-w EU import tariffs on battery electric vehicles may not substantially hinder the overall electrification process but may substantially reduce adoption in vulnerable markets. These findings highlight the uneven implications of broad trade interventions and underscore the importance of complementary policies to support a resilient and inclusive transition.

title: Distributional impacts of EU tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles across European markets
abstract: Published online: 10 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02105-7 Tariffs have been introduced in the EU on battery electric vehicles (EVs) imported from China. New research considers how these tariffs may lead to cost increases and reshape EV adoption across the EU.

title: Rigid polyanion architectures enable multiple ion-transport pathways and nanocluster dynamics in composite polymer battery electrolytes
abstract: Published online: 10 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41565-026-02228-8 Distinct lithium-ion conduction mechanisms in composite polymer battery electrolytes based on rigid polyanion architectures are identified and quantitatively characterized, providing insight into ion-transport design.

title: Coherent twins for manufacturing thick lithium-rich battery positive electrodes
abstract: Published online: 10 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41565-026-02221-1 Coherent twin boundaries enable three-dimensional Li-ion transport and reduce structural stress in Li-rich battery positive electrodes, enabling effective operation of thick electrodes in Li-ion pouch cells.

title: Multi-Physics Coupled Modeling and Failure Deduction of Large-Capacity LFP Batteries During Overcharge-Induced <mark>Thermal Runaway</mark>
authors: Jun Xie, Yuanxin Bai, Yixiao Zhang, Yaxuan Liu, Kun Tian and Zhichao Tang
abstract: As energy storage scales up, overcharge-induced <mark>thermal runaway</mark> (TR) in large-capacity batteries poses critical risks. This study develops a 3D multiphysics model for 280 Ah prismatic <mark>lithium</mark> iron phosphate (LFP) batteries, coupling electrochemistry, heat conduction, and side reactions while incorporating gas-pressure-structural response dynamics. To resolve high-dimensional parameter identification challenges, a staged strategy using Bayesian optimization and cross-dimensional mapping is proposed, ensuring consistency between 1D and 3D models while minimizing computational costs.
